# Infrastructure Design

Generated by `/gaia-infra-design` for the AcmeAPI service.

sidecar_decision: devops-sidecar/infrastructure-decisions.md

## Environments

| Environment | Purpose | Access Policy | Parity |
|-------------|---------|---------------|--------|
| dev | Developer iteration | Engineering team read-write | Minimal — single-AZ |
| staging | Pre-production validation | Eng + QA read-write, product read | High parity with production |
| production | End-user serving | SRE on-call only, IAM-gated writes | Baseline |

Environment parity strategy: staging mirrors production topology, data volumes, and IAM roles; differences are limited to data residency and scale factor. Promotion gates require passing unit, integration, and canary tests before moving dev → staging → production.

## Deployment

- Container orchestration: Kubernetes (EKS) with per-service Deployments and HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources.
- Load balancing: AWS ALB terminates TLS at the edge, routing to service-level Ingress controllers; internal service-to-service traffic uses an Istio service mesh.
- Auto-scaling: HPA triggers on CPU > 70% and a custom RPS metric; cluster-autoscaler grows the node group between 3 and 24 nodes.
- Networking: VPC with three private subnets across three AZs, public subnets only for the ALB; security groups restrict inter-service traffic to the service mesh. CloudFront CDN fronts public read traffic for static assets.
- Horizontal scaling is the default; vertical scaling is used only for stateful nodes.

## IaC

- IaC tool: Terraform 1.6 with the HashiCorp AWS provider. Rationale: team expertise and existing module ecosystem.
- Module structure mirrors the service boundary: one top-level module per service (auth-service, user-service, gateway), each composing shared primitives (vpc, k8s-namespace, ecr-repo).
- State management strategy: remote state stored in S3 with DynamoDB state locking; per-environment workspace isolation; state encryption via KMS.
- Naming conventions: `{service}-{env}-{resource}` applied via a shared locals module.
- Secrets management: AWS Secrets Manager referenced by ARN from Terraform; never inlined.

## Observability

- Logging strategy: structured JSON logs shipped via Fluent Bit to CloudWatch Logs with 30-day hot retention and 1-year S3 archive.
- Metrics and dashboards: Prometheus scrapes each service; Grafana dashboards cover RED (Rate/Errors/Duration), infra saturation, and SLO burn-down.
- Distributed tracing: OpenTelemetry SDK in every service emits spans with W3C correlation IDs; traces are exported to Tempo.
- Alerting and escalation: SLO-based alerts route through PagerDuty to the SRE on-call rotation; Sev-1 pages the primary, Sev-2 notifies #ops. Alerting thresholds follow Google SRE multi-window burn-rate guidance.
- On-call rotation: primary + secondary, weekly handoff; runbook links embedded in every alert payload.

## Decision Rationale

Every infrastructure decision above is traced back to the architecture document's component inventory; each choice is recorded in the devops-sidecar decision log for durable audit.
